question.text: A retiree who has no valid Will and dies intestate will have their assets distributed according to:

answer.text: The wishes expressed verbally to family members
The nominations made in financial accounts
The applicable succession law based on their religion

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Intestate succession follows the applicable Personal Law based on the deceased's religion. The Hindu Succession Act, Indian Succession Act, or Muslim Personal Law will determine how assets are distributed among legal heirs. **The Trap:** Nominations (Option B) facilitate transfer but do not determine ownership. Verbal wishes (Option A) have no legal standing without a valid Will. **Cross-Exam Context:** Intestate succession is covered in Series X-B and XVII. Understanding this helps advisers emphasize the importance of Will preparation. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Use the consequences of intestate succession as a motivator for clients to prepare their Wills. Without a Will, assets may not go to intended beneficiaries.
